title: ASTS Stock Jumps After FCC Clears 223 Additional Satellites
text: ASTS stock surged following FCC approval to deploy 223 additional low-Earth-orbit satellites as part of its space-based cellular broadband constellation for supplemental coverage from space and direct-to-device connectivity. The FCC order authorizes AST SpaceMobile to deploy the satellites with spectrum leasing deals involving AT&T, Verizon, and FirstNet, allowing AST SpaceMobile to operate satellite-to-smartphone services across portions of the 700 MHz and 800 MHz cellular bands in the continental U.S. In 2024, Verizon signed an agreement with AST SpaceMobile committing $20 million prepaid service-revenue payment and $45 million prepayment tied to regulatory approvals. The latest FCC approval increases the likelihood of triggering the remaining $45 million prepayment linked to commercialization milestones. AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy between 45 and 60 satellites in 2026 to enable continuous broadband service in initial U.S. target markets, with a target of up to 90 satellites potentially achievable by 2027.
